# HPC Storage {#sec-hpc-storage}

This chapter covers the Savio-specific storage architecture. For general data management practices (version control, documentation, reproducibility), see @sec-data-code.

## Directory structure

### Home directories (30 GB per user) {#sec-home}

**Path:** `/global/home/users/<your_username>`

Your home directory is the right place for personal scripts, git repositories, job scripts, and software configuration files. Home directories are backed up regularly.

::: {.callout-warning}
Do **not** store large datasets, intermediate files, or anything you wish to share with the lab in your home directory.
:::

### Group directory (200 GB shared)

**Path:** `/home/groups/co_carleton`

The group directory is the central location for shared software and tools. The recommended layout is:

```
/home/groups/co_carleton/
├── software/     # Shared Python envs, containers
├── scripts/      # Common analysis scripts and workflows
├── docs/         # Shared documentation and protocols
└── configs/      # Shared configuration files
```

::: {.callout-note}
This directory is not yet fully set up. Check the **#computing** Slack channel for updates.
:::

### Scratch space (9 PB shared across campus)

Scratch is high-performance temporary storage ideal for active computations, intermediate results, and temporary large files.

::: {.callout-important}
Scratch is **not backed up**. Data can be purged after 60–90 days of inactivity. Move important results to permanent storage immediately after your job completes.
:::

### Project storage (112 TB allocation) {#sec-project-storage}

**Path:** `/global/scratch/projects/co_carleton`

This is the primary location for lab data. It is visible and accessible to all members of the `co_carleton` group.

::: {.callout-tip}
If you work with sensitive data, contact Tamma, Cullen, or the BRC team to discuss options — including the secure storage tier.
:::

The 112 TB allocation is shared with the department. Lab members must work within the `carleton_colab` subfolder:

```
/global/scratch/projects/co_carleton/
├── department/
│   └── ...
└── carleton_colab/
    ├── datasets/
    │   ├── raw/
    │   ├── processed/
    │   └── shared/
    └── projects/
        └── project_name/
            ├── data/
            ├── results/
            └── analysis/
```

### Secure storage (1 TB allocation)

**Paths:**

- `/global/home/groups/pl1data/pl1_co_carleton_secure`
- `/global/scratch/p2p3/pl1_co_carleton_secure`

For data that must meet strict encryption regulations or requirements. Not yet set up.

## Avoiding duplication on Savio

112 TB sounds like a lot, but large geospatial datasets fill it up fast. Before downloading a new dataset (e.g., ERA5, CHIRPS, CRU), post in **#computing** on Slack to check whether someone already has it. If the dataset might be useful to others, place it in `carleton_colab/datasets/` rather than inside your project folder. Use symbolic links from project directories to avoid maintaining multiple copies.

## Backups

Only home directories are backed up. For critical data, consider maintaining external backups via cloud services or local drives.

## Shared dataset best practices

- Set appropriate group permissions on shared files.
- Keep the lab data registry up to date with dataset location, owner, permissions, size, and creation date.
- Every dataset should include a README describing its source, collection date, processing steps, file formats, and naming conventions.
